stemHtml: A student pilot is flying with an instructor in a single engine aircraft. The instructor is demonstrating the effects of a stall shortly after take-off, specifically the effects of a stall during a turn. What are the initial effects of the stall in this scenario, and correct recovery procedures?

options.a: Increased yaw towards the stalled wing; recover by reducing the angle of attack and applying the opposite rudder
options.b: Increased roll towards the stalled wing; recover by increasing power and rolling wing level
options.c: Increased pitch up; recover by reducing power and leveling the wings
options.d: Decreased yaw towards the stalled wing; recover by applying opposite aileron and reducing power
